 {"id":"beads_rust-oz14","title":"[MEDIUM] comment ordering is unstable for same-second timestamps","description":"Root cause: src/storage/sqlite.rs:4967,4997,5698 order comments only by created_at, but add_comment stores CURRENT_TIMESTAMP with second precision; multiple comments in the same second can be returned/exported in storage-engine order instead of insertion order. Repro: insert two comments with the same created_at and descending ids, then get_comments/get_all_comments can emit the higher id first depending on the query plan. Proposed fix: add id ASC as a tie-breaker for per-issue, batch, and export comment queries. -{"id":"beads_rust-p7xo","title":"Dead code behind #[allow(dead_code)]: 3 functions/structs with zero callers","description":"/mock-code-finder scan found 3 genuine dead-code items annotated with #[allow(dead_code)] that have full implementations but zero callers anywhere in src/:

1. **src/cli/commands/audit.rs:443** — `no_fields_provided(args: &AuditRecordArgs) -> bool`
   Checks whether all audit record fields are empty/None. Was likely intended as a validation guard before recording, but `execute_audit_record()` never calls it. The sibling `is_empty_opt()` IS used (by `clean_opt`).
   Remediation: wire into audit record validation or delete.

2. **src/storage/sqlite.rs:5297** — `collect_descendant_ids(&self, parent_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>>`
   Full BFS traversal over parent_id foreign keys. Implemented but never called — the codebase currently has no need to walk the subtree. Only one hit in grep.
   Remediation: delete; re-implement if subtree queries are needed later.

3. **src/sync/mod.rs:7914+7933** — `progress_bar()` and `progress_spinner()`
   Module-private helpers that duplicate `util::progress::{create_progress_bar, create_spinner}`, which are the functions actually imported and used (sync/mod.rs:24, lines 1733/3328/3589).
   Remediation: delete both; util::progress is the canonical source.

Two other `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations were verified as FALSE POSITIVES:
- `close.rs:103 CloseExecution` — all 4 fields are used (lines 384-409)
- `sync.rs:74 SyncPathPolicy` — struct has 9 references; annotation covers specific fields

None of these dead items affect correctness.  {"id":"beads_rust-p8kw","title":"Merge --force-jsonl can resurrect local tombstones","description":"Round 2 review finding: execute_merge calls three_way_merge with tombstones=None, and three_way_merge's tombstone guard only skips resurrection when the tombstoned ID is absent from local. If the local DB contains a tombstone and JSONL contains a non-tombstone record for the same ID, --force-jsonl can choose the JSONL winner and resurrect a deleted issue. Import paths block this, so merge should preserve tombstone protection too.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:21:28.395652971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:30:31.579625719Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:30:31.579332100Z","close_reason":"Merge now passes local tombstone IDs into the three-way merge engine and blocks non-tombstone JSONL winners from resurrecting them; unit and e2e regressions cover force-jsonl.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-pfx","title":"Conformance Test Harness","description":"# Conformance Test Harness

## Current State: IMPLEMENTED
The test harness is fully implemented in tests/conformance.rs:

### Infrastructure Complete
- ConformanceWorkspace struct with paired br/bd directories
- Logging to files under logs/ directory
- Multiple CompareMode variants:
  - ExactJson
  - NormalizedJson (handles timestamps, IDs)
  - ContainsFields
  - ExitCodeOnly
  - ArrayUnordered
  - FieldsExcluded
  - StructureOnly

### Utilities Complete
- normalize_json() for timestamp/ID normalization
- diff_json() for human-readable diff output
- extract_json_payload() for JSON extraction

### Benchmark Infrastructure Complete
- BenchmarkConfig (warmup_runs, timed_runs, outlier_threshold)
- TimingStats (mean, median, p95, std_dev)
- run_benchmark() with outlier filtering

### Test Scenarios Framework
- TestScenario struct for reusable scenarios
- Pre-defined scenarios in scenarios module

## Remaining Work
- None - harness is feature complete

 {"id":"beads_rust-ykb3","title":"CI Integration: Conformance Test Automation","description":"# CI Integration: Conformance Test Automation

## Purpose
Integrate the comprehensive conformance test suite into CI/CD pipeline to catch br/bd divergence automatically on every PR. This ensures the isomorphism guarantee is maintained throughout development.

## Requirements

### 1. GitHub Actions Workflow

Create `.github/workflows/conformance.yml`:

```yaml
name: Conformance Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
  RUST_LOG: beads_rust=debug

jobs:
  conformance:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust (nightly)
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@nightly
        with:
          components: rustfmt, clippy
          
      - name: Cache Cargo
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/bin/
            ~/.cargo/registry/index/
            ~/.cargo/registry/cache/
            ~/.cargo/git/db/
            target/
          key: conformance-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          
      - name: Install Go
        uses: actions/setup-go@v4
        with:
          go-version: '1.21'
          
      - name: Install bd (Go beads)
        run: |
          go install github.com/steveyegge/beads/cmd/bd@latest
          echo "$(go env GOPATH)/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
          
      - name: Verify bd installation
        run: bd --version
        
      - name: Build br (release)
        run: cargo build --release
        
      - name: Run Conformance Tests
        run: |
          cargo test conformance --release -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 2>&1 | tee conformance.log
        
      - name: Generate Test Report
        if: always()
        run: |
          # Parse test output and generate JUnit XML
          cargo test conformance --release -- --format=junit > conformance-results.xml || true
          
      - name: Upload Test Results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: conformance-results
          path: |
            conformance.log
            conformance-results.xml
            target/conformance-logs/
          retention-days: 30
          
      - name: Publish Test Report
        if: always()
        uses: mikepenz/action-junit-report@v4
        with:
          report_paths: 'conformance-results.xml'
          fail_on_failure: true
          include_passed: true

  benchmark-gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    needs: conformance
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          # Same setup as conformance job
          
      - name: Run Benchmark Comparison
        run: |
          cargo run --release --bin br-bench -- \
            --format=markdown \
            --baseline=.github/benchmark-baseline.json \
            --fail-on-regression=15% \
            > benchmark-report.md
            
      - name: Comment PR with Results
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const report = fs.readFileSync('benchmark-report.md', 'utf8');
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              body: report
            });
```

### 2. Test Result Reporting

**JUnit XML Generation**:
```rust
// In tests/conformance/report.rs
pub fn generate_junit_xml(results: &[TestResult]) -> String {
    let mut xml = String::from(r#"<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>"#);
    xml.push_str(&format!(
        r#"<testsuite name="conformance" tests="{}" failures="{}" time="{}">"#,
        results.len(),
        results.iter().filter(|r| !r.passed).count(),
        results.iter().map(|r| r.duration.as_secs_f64()).sum::<f64>()
    ));
    
    for result in results {
        xml.push_str(&format!(
            r#"<testcase name="{}" classname="conformance" time="{}">"#,
            result.name,
            result.duration.as_secs_f64()
        ));
        if !result.passed {
            xml.push_str(&format!(
                r#"<failure message="{}"><![CDATA[{}]]></failure>"#,
                result.error_message.as_deref().unwrap_or(""),
                result.diff.as_deref().unwrap_or("")
            ));
        }
        xml.push_str("</testcase>");
    }
    
    xml.push_str("</testsuite>");
    xml
}
```

**Summary PR Comment**:
```markdown
## 🧪 Conformance Test Results

| Category | Passed | Failed | Skipped |
|----------|--------|--------|---------|
| CRUD | 56/56 | 0 | 0 |
| Dependencies | 29/29 | 0 | 0 |
| Sync | 32/32 | 0 | 0 |
| Utility | 39/39 | 0 | 0 |
| **Total** | **156/156** | **0** | **0** |

### ⏱️ Performance Summary
- Average test duration: 0.45s
- Total suite time: 2m 15s
- Slowest test: `test_sync_1000_issues` (12.3s)

<details>
<summary>View detailed log</summary>

[Full log output here]

</details>
```

### 3. Failure Handling

On conformance failure, CI must:

1. **Capture full outputs**:
```bash
# Save both br and bd outputs
mkdir -p artifacts/failures/$TEST_NAME
cp br_output.json artifacts/failures/$TEST_NAME/
cp bd_output.json artifacts/failures/$TEST_NAME/
```

2. **Generate visual diff**:
```rust
fn generate_diff_html(br: &str, bd: &str) -> String {
    // Use similar-asserts or custom diff for colored output
}
```

3. **Create actionable error**:
```
❌ Conformance failure: test_create_with_priority

Expected (bd):
  {"title": "Test", "priority": 1, "status": "open"}
  
Actual (br):
  {"title": "Test", "priority": 1, "status": "Open"}  // Note: "Open" vs "open"
  
Difference:
  - status: "open"
  + status: "Open"
  
Suggestion: Check src/model/status.rs for case sensitivity in serialization
```

4. **Archive comprehensive logs**:
```
artifacts/
├── conformance.log          # Full test output
├── conformance-results.xml  # JUnit XML
├── failures/
│   └── test_create_with_priority/
│       ├── br_output.json
│       ├── bd_output.json
│       ├── diff.html
│       └── test.log
└── timing/
    └── performance.csv
```

### 4. Performance Gate (Optional)

**Regression Detection**:
```yaml
- name: Check for Performance Regression
  run: |
    NEW_MEDIAN=$(jq '.summary.median_speedup' benchmark-results.json)
    OLD_MEDIAN=$(jq '.summary.median_speedup' .github/benchmark-baseline.json)
    
    REGRESSION=$(echo "scale=2; ($OLD_MEDIAN - $NEW_MEDIAN) / $OLD_MEDIAN * 100" | bc)
    
    if [ $(echo "$REGRESSION > 15" | bc) -eq 1 ]; then
      echo "::error::Performance regression detected: ${REGRESSION}%"
      exit 1
    fi
```

### 5. Detailed Logging Infrastructure

**Log Levels**:
```rust
pub enum LogLevel {
    Error,    // Only failures
    Warn,     // Failures + warnings
    Info,     // + test progress
    Debug,    // + command details
    Trace,    // + raw outputs
}
```

**Structured Log Format**:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-17T10:00:00.123Z",
  "level": "INFO",
  "test": "conformance_create_basic",
  "phase": "execute",
  "message": "Running br create",
  "context": {
    "command": ["create", "Test issue", "--json"],
    "workspace": "/tmp/conformance_abc123/br_workspace"
  }
}
```

**Log Aggregation**:
```rust
pub struct TestRunSummary {
    pub total_tests: usize,
    pub passed: usize,
    pub failed: usize,
    pub skipped: usize,
    pub total_duration: Duration,
    pub slowest_tests: Vec<(String, Duration)>,
    pub failures: Vec<TestFailure>,
}
```

## Implementation Tasks

1. [ ] Create GitHub Actions workflow file
2. [ ] Add JUnit XML reporter to conformance tests
3. [ ] Configure artifact collection
4. [ ] Add PR comment bot for results summary
5. [ ] Implement failure diff generation
6. [ ] Add benchmark gate (optional)
7. [ ] Document CI requirements in CONTRIBUTING.md
8. [ ] Add status badge to README

## Files to Create/Modify
- `.github/workflows/conformance.yml` - Main workflow
- `.github/workflows/benchmark.yml` - Benchmark comparison (optional)
- `.github/benchmark-baseline.json` - Performance baseline
- `tests/conformance/report.rs` - JUnit XML generation
- `README.md` - Add CI badge

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI runs on every PR
- [ ] Clear pass/fail status visible in PR checks
- [ ] Detailed logs available as downloadable artifacts
- [ ] JUnit XML integrates with GitHub test reporting
- [ ] Failure messages are actionable (show diff, suggest fix)
- [ ] README has CI status badge
- [ ] Documentation for local CI reproduction

## Notes
- Tests run with `--test-threads=1` to avoid parallelism issues with bd
- Consider separate workflow for nightly comprehensive runs
- Cache Cargo and Go dependencies for faster CI","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:13:33.510001730Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:40.875661117Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:40.875661117Z","close_reason":"Overlaps with beads_rust-na7 (CI/CD Pipeline with GitHub Actions) which is in_progress","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-ykb3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-egz8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ykm","title":"where Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br where` command to show the location of the beads directory and database. Useful for debugging path issues and understanding project structure.

## CLI Interface
```
br where [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --db                      Show database path only
  --jsonl                   Show JSONL directory only
  --config                  Show config file path only
  --all                     Show all paths (default)
  --json                    Output as JSON
```

## Technical Requirements

### Path Discovery
```rust
fn discover_paths() -> Result<BeadsPaths> {
    let beads_dir = discover_beads_dir()?;  // Walk up to find .beads/
    let db_path = beads_dir.join(format\!("{}.db", get_prefix()?));
    let jsonl_dir = beads_dir.clone();
    let project_config = beads_dir.join("config.yaml");
    let user_config = dirs::config_dir()
        .map(|d| d.join("br/config.yaml"));
    
    Ok(BeadsPaths {
        beads_dir,
        db_path,
        jsonl_dir,
        project_config,
        user_config,
    })
}

fn discover_beads_dir() -> Result<PathBuf> {
    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?;
    let mut dir = cwd.as_path();
    
    loop {
        let beads = dir.join(".beads");
        if beads.is_dir() {
            return Ok(beads);
        }
        dir = dir.parent()
            .ok_or_else(|| BeadsError::NotInBeadsProject)?;
    }
}
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable (default)
```
Beads Project Paths:
  .beads directory:  /home/user/project/.beads
  Database:          /home/user/project/.beads/bd.db
  JSONL directory:   /home/user/project/.beads
  Project config:    /home/user/project/.beads/config.yaml (exists)
  User config:       /home/user/.config/br/config.yaml (not found)
```

### --db (single path)
```
/home/user/project/.beads/bd.db
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "beads_dir": "/home/user/project/.beads",
  "db_path": "/home/user/project/.beads/bd.db",
  "jsonl_dir": "/home/user/project/.beads",
  "project_config": "/home/user/project/.beads/config.yaml",
  "project_config_exists": true,
  "user_config": "/home/user/.config/br/config.yaml",
  "user_config_exists": false
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Discover .beads directory by walking up from cwd
- [ ] Show database path
- [ ] Show JSONL directory path
- [ ] Show config file paths with existence check
- [ ] Individual path flags (--db, --jsonl, --config)
- [ ] Error if not in a beads project
- [ ] Human and JSON output

## Unit Tests
- Finds .beads in current directory
- Finds .beads in parent directory
- Error when not in beads project
- Each path flag outputs single path
- JSON contains all paths
- Existence flags correct

## Dependencies
- Configuration system (for paths)

## Rationale
The where command helps users and scripts locate beads resources.  {"id":"beads_rust-ynn","title":"EPIC: Documentation & Onboarding Excellence","description":"# Documentation & Onboarding Excellence

## Background & Rationale

Based on research of developer experience best practices and the patterns observed in mature tools (xf, cass, beads_viewer, ACFS), br needs comprehensive documentation that enables both human developers and AI coding agents to use it effectively.

### Why This Matters
- No README.md currently exists (critical gap)
- AI agents rely heavily on AGENTS.md for context
- One-liner installation needs clear quick-start docs
- Agent integration requires specific guidance
- Users need troubleshooting resources

## Goals
Create world-class documentation that enables zero-friction onboarding for both human users and AI coding agents, with clear examples and comprehensive reference material.

## Deliverables

### 1. README.md (Project Landing Page)
- One-liner installation command
- TL;DR section (30-second overview)
- Feature table with status indicators
- Quick example (practical usage)
- Architecture overview (high-level)
- Agent integration blurb
- Contributing guide link
- License

### 2. AGENTS.md Enhancement
- Current file is good but needs:
  - More examples of common agent workflows
  - Robot mode flag documentation
  - Error handling guidance for agents
  - MCP integration instructions (when available)

### 3. Installation Guide (docs/INSTALLING.md)
- All installation methods:
  - One-liner script
  - Homebrew
  - Scoop (Windows)
  - Cargo install
  - From source
- Platform-specific notes
- Proxy configuration
- Troubleshooting common issues

### 4. CLI Reference (docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md)
- Every command with examples
- All flags and options
- JSON output schemas
- Exit codes
- Environment variables

### 5. Agent Integration Guide (docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md)
- Supported AI coding agents
- Configuration for each agent type
- Robot mode flags reference
- JSON output parsing examples
- Workflow examples (ready → claim → work → close)
- Error handling patterns
- MCP server setup (when available)

### 6. Troubleshooting Guide (docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md)
- Common errors and solutions
- Database recovery
- Sync conflict resolution
- Performance issues
- Debug logging

### 7. Architecture Documentation (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Module overview
- Data flow diagrams
- SQLite schema
- JSONL format specification
- Extension points

## Documentation Standards

### Structure per file
1. Quick summary (1-2 sentences)
2. Table of contents for long docs
3. Examples for every concept
4. Links to related docs
5. Last updated date

### Code Examples
- All examples must be tested/working
- Include both human and JSON output
- Show error cases too

### Agent-Friendly Formatting
- Use consistent markdown headers
- Keep paragraphs concise
- Use tables for reference data
- Include JSON schemas where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- README.md exists with all sections
- AGENTS.md enhanced with agent workflows
- All docs pass markdown lint
- Examples are tested and work
- Docs are discoverable (linked from README)
- Search-friendly (good headings, keywords)

## References
- xf README structure
- cass documentation patterns
- Anthropic Claude Code docs
- Rust API documentation guidelines

## Dependencies
- None (can start immediately)

 {"id":"beads_rust-z1bb","title":"Integrate rich output into init command","description":"## Command: br init [PREFIX]

### Traffic Level: LOW (once per project)
Project initialization - first impression matters!

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/init.rs
Output: Success message with directory path

### Integration Steps
1. Show initialization progress with spinner
2. Display created structure visually
3. Add 'next steps' suggestions

### Visual Enhancement
```
╭─ Beads Initialized ─────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  ✓ Created .beads/ directory                        │
│  ✓ Initialized SQLite database                      │
│  ✓ Set issue prefix: 'beads_rust'                   │
│                                                     │
│  📁 .beads/                                         │
│     ├── beads.db (SQLite database)                  │
│     └── issues.jsonl (portable backup)              │
│                                                     │
│  Next steps:                                        │
│    br create "My first issue"                       │
│    br list                                          │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/init_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_init_success_output() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_init_success("beads_rust", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Initialized"));
    assert!(output.contains("beads_rust"));
}

#[test]
fn test_init_shows_structure() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_init_success("test", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains(".beads"));
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/init_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Init Command ==="

# Test in temp directory
TESTDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$TESTDIR"
trap 'rm -rf "$TESTDIR"' EXIT

log_step "Testing br init"
INIT_OUTPUT=$(br init --prefix test 2>&1)
assert_contains "$INIT_OUTPUT" "Initialized"
assert_file_exists ".beads/beads.db"
log_pass "br init works"

log_step "Testing re-init protection"
REINIT_OUTPUT=$(br init 2>&1 || true)
# Should warn or error
log_pass "Re-init handled"

log_success "=== Init command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

 {"id":"beads_rust-z39t","title":"Implement DependencyTree component","description":"### Purpose
Visualizes issue dependency graphs for `dep tree`, `dep cycles`, and embedded dependency sections. Critical for understanding blocking relationships.

### File Location
`src/format/components/dep_tree.rs`

### CRITICAL: Integration with Existing Code
Must integrate with existing patterns:
- Use Theme for node coloring
- Preserve existing TreeNode output structure from `src/format/output.rs`
- Integrate with existing `dep tree` JSON output

### API Design
```rust
use crate::format::{OutputContext, Theme};
use crate::format::output::TreeNode;
use crate::storage::Storage;
use crate::model::Issue;

pub struct DependencyTree<'a> {
    root: &'a Issue,
    storage: &'a dyn Storage,
    ctx: &'a OutputContext,
    max_depth: Option<usize>,
    direction: TreeDirection,
}

pub enum TreeDirection {
    Blockers,    // Show what this blocks
    BlockedBy,   // Show what blocks this
    Both,        // Bidirectional view
}

impl<'a> DependencyTree<'a> {
    pub fn new(root: &'a Issue, storage: &'a dyn Storage, ctx: &'a OutputContext) -> Self;
    pub fn direction(mut self, dir: TreeDirection) -> Self;
    pub fn max_depth(mut self, depth: usize) -> Self;
    pub fn render(&self) -> Result<()>;
    pub fn render_to_string(&self) -> Result<String>;  // For testing
}
```

### Visual Structure
```
beads_rust-abc1 [open] Fix auth bug
├── blocks:
│   ├── beads_rust-def2 [open] Update login page
│   │   └── beads_rust-ghi3 [closed] Add tests
│   └── beads_rust-jkl4 [open] Deploy to staging
└── blocked-by:
    └── beads_rust-mno5 [in-progress] Database migration
        └── beads_rust-pqr6 [closed] Schema design
```

### Mode Behavior
- Rich: Colored tree with status indicators
- Plain: ASCII tree with plain status text
- JSON: TreeNode array (UNCHANGED from current)
- Quiet: Just IDs in tree order

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/format/dep_tree_tests.rs

```rust
use beads_rust::format::components::{DependencyTree, TreeDirection};
use beads_rust::format::OutputContext;
use beads_rust::model::{Issue, Status};

fn create_tree_issues() -> (Issue, Vec<Issue>) {
    let root = Issue {
        id: "root".to_string(),
        title: "Root issue".to_string(),
        status: Status::Open,
        dependencies: vec![],
        ..Default::default()
    };
    let child1 = Issue { id: "child1".to_string(), .. };
    let child2 = Issue { id: "child2".to_string(), .. };
    (root, vec![child1, child2])
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_renders_root() {
    let (root, _) = create_tree_issues();
    let storage = MockStorage::new();
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&root, &storage, &ctx);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    assert!(output.contains("root"));
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_renders_children() {
    let (root, deps) = create_tree_issues();
    let storage = MockStorage::with_deps(&root, &deps);
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&root, &storage, &ctx)
        .direction(TreeDirection::Blockers);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    assert!(output.contains("child1"));
    assert!(output.contains("child2"));
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_has_tree_drawing_chars() {
    let (root, deps) = create_tree_issues();
    let storage = MockStorage::with_deps(&root, &deps);
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&root, &storage, &ctx)
        .direction(TreeDirection::Blockers);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    assert!(output.contains("├") || output.contains("└"));
    assert!(output.contains("│") || output.contains("   "));
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_respects_max_depth() {
    let storage = MockStorage::with_deep_tree(5);
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&storage.root(), &storage, &ctx)
        .max_depth(2);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    // Should only show 2 levels + truncation indicator
    assert!(output.contains("...") || !output.contains("level-3"));
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_bidirectional() {
    let (root, _) = create_tree_issues();
    let storage = MockStorage::with_bidirectional_deps();
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&root, &storage, &ctx)
        .direction(TreeDirection::Both);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    assert!(output.contains("blocks"));
    assert!(output.contains("blocked-by"));
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_status_indicators() {
    let storage = MockStorage::with_varied_statuses();
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&storage.root(), &storage, &ctx);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    assert!(output.contains("open") || output.contains("○"));
    assert!(output.contains("closed") || output.contains("✓"));
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_plain_no_ansi() {
    let storage = MockStorage::with_deps();
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&storage.root(), &storage, &ctx);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    assert!(!contains_ansi_codes(&output));
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_rich_has_colors() {
    let storage = MockStorage::with_deps();
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&storage.root(), &storage, &ctx);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    // Rich mode should have ANSI codes for colors
    assert!(contains_ansi_codes(&output));
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_json_mode_empty() {
    let storage = MockStorage::with_deps();
    let ctx = OutputContext::json();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&storage.root(), &storage, &ctx);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    // JSON mode handled separately, tree should not render
    assert!(output.is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn test_tree_empty_deps() {
    let root = Issue { id: "lonely".to_string(), .. };
    let storage = MockStorage::new();
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&root, &storage, &ctx);
    let output = tree.render_to_string().unwrap();
    // Should handle gracefully
    assert!(output.contains("lonely"));
}
```

### Snapshot Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn snapshot_tree_plain() {
    let storage = MockStorage::with_standard_tree();
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&storage.root(), &storage, &ctx);
    insta::assert_snapshot!(tree.render_to_string().unwrap());
}

#[test]
fn snapshot_tree_rich() {
    let storage = MockStorage::with_standard_tree();
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let tree = DependencyTree::new(&storage.root(), &storage, &ctx);
    insta::assert_snapshot!(strip_ansi(&tree.render_to_string().unwrap()));
}
```

### E2E Verification
Tested indirectly through:
- tests/e2e/dep_e2e.sh (dep tree command)
- tests/e2e/show_e2e.sh (dependency section)

### Logging Requirements
- Debug log tree depth and direction
- Debug log cycle detection if enabled
- Info log if tree exceeds 100 nodes (performance warning)

Dependencies:
  -> beads_rust-38mz (blocks) - Implement OutputContext with mode detection
  -> beads_rust-2d42 (parent-child) - Phase 2: Core Components - Reusable rich output building blocks
  -> beads_rust-2f4x (blocks) - Phase 1: Foundation Layer - Output abstraction infrastructure

Dependents:
  <- beads_rust-1g8e (blocks) - Integrate rich output into dep subcommands
  <- beads_rust-zcii (blocks) - Integrate rich output into graph command

### Edge Cases

#### Empty Dependency Tree
When issue has no dependencies:
- Show concise message: "No dependencies"
- Don't render empty tree visualization

#### Very Deep Tree (depth > 10)
- Collapse nodes beyond depth 10 with "... (N more levels)"
- Warn user: `debug!(depth, "Deep dependency tree")`
- Prevent exponential rendering time

#### Circular Dependencies
- Detect cycles before rendering
- Mark circular refs with ⟲ symbol
- Show warning: "Circular dependency detected: A → B → A"

#### Wide Tree (many siblings)
- Limit siblings shown to 10 per level
- Show "+ N more at this level" link
- Add `--full` flag to show all

#### Mixed Status Dependencies
- Closed deps should be dimmed
- In-progress deps highlighted
- Blocked deps shown in red
- Ready deps shown in green

#### Cross-Project Dependencies
If system ever supports cross-project deps:
- Prefix with project slug
- Different color for external","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:29:58.838480022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T05:52:10.023109963Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T05:52:10.022720379Z","close_reason":"Component fully implemented in src/output/components/","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-z39t","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-2d42","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-z39t","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-2f4x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-z39t","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-38mz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-zou7","title":"Snapshot testing with insta crate","description":"# Snapshot Testing with insta

## Overview
Add insta for snapshot testing CLI output stability.

## Dependencies
```toml
[dev-dependencies]
insta = { version = "1.34", features = ["json", "yaml"] }
```

## Snapshot Targets

### CLI Output (Human-Readable)
1. `br list` output format
2. `br show <id>` output format
3. `br ready` output format
4. `br blocked` output format
5. `br stats` output format
6. `br version` output format
7. Error message formats

### JSON Output
8. `br list --json` structure
9. `br show --json` structure
10. `br create --json` response
11. `br ready --json` structure

### Help Text
12. `br --help` output
13. `br create --help` output
14. Subcommand help texts

## Usage
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_output() {
    let output = run_br(&["list"]);
    insta::assert_snapshot!(output.stdout);
}
```

## Review Workflow
```bash
cargo insta test        # Run tests
cargo insta review      # Review changes
cargo insta accept      # Accept new snapshots
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] insta added to Cargo.toml
- [ ] 14+ snapshots for CLI outputs
- [ ] Snapshots committed to tests/snapshots/
- [ ] CI validates snapshots don't drift","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:28:55.968445825Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:14:09.004190121Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:14:09.004190121Z","close_reason":"Snapshot testing with insta is already fully implemented: tests/snapshots/ has 32 test functions across 4 files (cli_output, error_messages, json_output, jsonl_format) with 42 snapshot files.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-zou7","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-7kme","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"beads_rust-zs1f","title":"30+ integration tests pass without verifying behavior (assertion-free or let-discard)","description":"Systematic scan of tests/*.rs found 30+ test functions that pass without meaningful assertions. Unlike beads_rust-949m (src/ unit tests), these are integration/e2e tests that exercise real CLI and storage paths but only verify "doesn't crash" rather than correctness.

## Highest-impact gaps (assertions would catch real bugs):

### Storage list filter smoke tests (result discarded)
- **tests/storage_list_filters.rs:249** — `filter_by_all_priority_levels` loops through all priority levels but uses `let _ = storage.list_issues(&filters).unwrap()` — never checks that correct issues are returned
- **tests/storage_list_filters.rs:344** — `filter_by_all_issue_types` — same pattern

### E2E tests with zero output verification
- **tests/test_create_deps_colon.rs:4** — `test_create_deps_colon_title` creates an issue with colon in title + dependency, but only prints JSON output (lines 51-65) — never asserts the dependency was actually linked
- **tests/test_create_parent_standin.rs:4** — creates parent epic relationship but never verifies the relationship exists

### Conformance tests that only check exit codes
These tests claim to verify specific behavior but only call `.assert_success()`:
- **tests/conformance.rs:10244** — `conformance_close_sets_closed_at` — name claims to verify closed_at timestamp, only checks exit code
- **tests/conformance.rs:10468** — `conformance_reopen_clears_closed_at` — name claims to verify cleared timestamp, only checks exit code
- **tests/conformance.rs:11242-11360** — 4 graph conformance tests (no_deps, simple_dep, complex_deps, all_flag) — only check exit codes, never verify graph structure
- **tests/conformance.rs:11614,11677** — audit record/label conformance — only check exit codes

### E2E workspace scenario tests
- **tests/e2e_workspace_scenarios.rs:219** — `scenario_doctor_healthy_workspace` — only checks exit code, never verifies doctor output
- **tests/e2e_workspace_scenarios.rs:388** — `scenario_version_no_workspace_required` — only checks exit code

## Remediation approach:

For **storage filter tests**: replace `let _ =` with `let results = ...` and assert on result count + issue IDs.

For **conformance exit-code-only tests**: parse JSON output and assert on the specific field the test name claims to verify (e.g., `conformance_close_sets_closed_at` should parse JSON and assert `closed_at` is non-null).

For **e2e tests**: add at minimum one assertion on the output that validates the test's documented intent.

## Not filing for:
- Benchmark/perf tests (tests/bench_*.rs) — `let _ =` is acceptable for timing
- Proptest where prop_assert! is inside the proptest! macro (those are real assertions)
- Tests intentionally marked `#[ignore]`","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"debt","created_at":"2026-04-22T23:28:20.101857537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:28:20.101857537Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arch","testing"]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-zwzh","title":"Conformance: Label & Comment Commands","description":"# Conformance: Label & Comment Commands

## Purpose
Verify br vs bd JSON parity for label and comments commands.

## Current State
- label: 1 test exists (conformance_label_basic)
- comments: No dedicated conformance tests

## Test Specifications

### label Command (12 new tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_label_add_single | Add one label | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_label_add_multiple | Add multiple labels | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_label_remove | Remove label | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_label_list | List issue labels | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_label_list_all | List all labels in workspace | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_label_special_chars | Label with special chars | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_label_unicode | Label with unicode | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_label_duplicate | Add existing label | ExitCodeOnly |
| conformance_label_remove_nonexistent | Remove missing label | ExitCodeOnly |
| conformance_label_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_label_filter_issues | List issues by label | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_label_case_sensitivity | Case handling | NormalizedJson |

### comments Command (13 new tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_comments_add | Add comment | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_comments_list | List comments | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_comments_empty | No comments | ExactJson |
| conformance_comments_multiple | Many comments | ArrayUnordered |
| conformance_comments_markdown | Markdown in body | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_comments_unicode | Unicode content | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_comments_edit | Edit comment | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_comments_delete | Delete comment | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_comments_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_comments_issue_not_found | Invalid issue ID | ExitCodeOnly |
| conformance_comments_ordering | Chronological order | ContainsFields |
| conformance_comments_author | Author field set | ContainsFields |
| conformance_comments_timestamps | Created/updated times | StructureOnly |

## Logging Requirements
\`\`\`rust
// For each label/comment operation
info!("conformance_{}: issue_id={}", test_name, issue_id);
info!("conformance_{}: operation={}", test_name, operation);
info!("conformance_{}: input={:?}", test_name, input_data);
info!("conformance_{}: br_result={}", test_name, br_json);
info!("conformance_{}: bd_result={}", test_name, bd_json);
\`\`\`

## Test Data Fixtures
Create reusable fixtures for:
- Issue with 0/1/many labels
- Issue with 0/1/many comments
- Unicode test strings
- Markdown content samples

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 25 new conformance tests
- [ ] Label special character handling verified
- [ ] Comment ordering and timestamps verified
- [ ] All tests include structured logging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusCodeAgent","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:10:18.687818760Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T17:06:11.164534856Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T17:06:11.164534856Z","close_reason":"Implemented 23 conformance tests for label and comment commands (12 label tests + 11 comment tests).
